purpose: Internal cross-record audit of whether the one-task LH pilot supplies a warranted evidence path from intended use to a bounded validity claim. This exercises reusable machinery; it is not ECBD validation or a claim that skill-bench is limited to benchmark-program analysis.

artifacts.role: task, requirement, artifact, and check record

nodes.label: Advise a benchmark program lead whether to adopt, reject, or pilot skill-grounded scoring.
nodes.scope: One internal benchmark-program-management scenario; stakeholder framing is a builder hypothesis.

nodes.label: Evidence-grounded professional adoption analysis that distinguishes agreement, validity, and configured-system effects.
nodes.scope: Proposed construct only; no expert construct map, occupational frame, or external professional criterion exists.

nodes.label: Reconcile aggregate inter-judge agreement with weak individual human/automated concordance.
nodes.scope: One evidence-backed requirement instantiated from one reviewed benchmark paper.

nodes.label: CSV evidence matrix and Markdown recommendation memo declared before execution.
nodes.scope: Final-artifact paths and acceptance criteria are declared, but no typed trace/workflow evidence view or expert-approved representation sufficiency is declared for this pilot.

nodes.label: Private contradiction-reconciliation check over the evidence matrix and memo.
nodes.scope: Human-judge interface only; the executable independent claim calibrator is internal regex/evidence-group calibration, not expert adjudication.

nodes.label: Exact deterministic emission rate for eight internally planted failed cells.
nodes.scope: Eight co-authored synthetic case-by-check cells only; not grader accuracy, agent capability, or a professional population estimate.

nodes.label: Evaluated agents can perform evidence-grounded professional adoption analysis.
nodes.scope: Explicitly unsupported construct claim in the existing validity ladder.

edges.warrant: A useful adoption decision should depend on calibrated interpretation of conflicting validity and effect evidence rather than fluent summary alone.
edges.scope: The source review supports the evidence distinction; the role, artifact, and decision-use mapping remain unvalidated builder adaptations.
edges.claim_consequence: Does not license professional construct relevance or usefulness until the intended use and construct are expert/stakeholder reviewed.

edges.warrant: Reconciliation of aggregate agreement with individual concordance is one observable boundary of calibrated evidence interpretation.
edges.scope: Content relevance for one reviewed-source contradiction; no evidence of construct coverage or discrimination from generic caution.
edges.claim_consequence: A pass may be criterion evidence for this item but cannot establish the broader professional construct.

edges.warrant: A row-level evidence matrix and recommendation memo could expose whether agreement and validity claims are represented at compatible scope.
edges.scope: Final artifacts are predeclared, but representation sufficiency, workflow observability, and professional conventions have not been independently reviewed.
edges.claim_consequence: Absent a sufficient response evidence view, workflow and professional-quality inferences fail closed.

edges.warrant: The declared check can inspect both final artifacts for scoped treatment of E01-E06 and E10.
edges.scope: Check definition has an evidence view, but its authoritative grader is interface-only and uncalibrated by experts.
edges.claim_consequence: Observed internal-calibrator outcomes cannot be relabeled as expert judgment or professional correctness.

edges.warrant: The metric exactly accumulates planted deterministic check outcomes, but that finite calibration observable is not the human professional criterion named upstream.
edges.scope: The metric is complete for eight synthetic cells only; the professional human check has no population estimate.
edges.claim_consequence: The exact 8/8 result supports regression behavior only and cannot accumulate into professional capability evidence.

edges.warrant: No warrant currently bridges co-authored planted-failure emission to the latent capability of professional adoption analysis.
edges.scope: No valid matched agent population, expert criterion, construct network, or cross-task transfer study exists.
edges.claim_consequence: Professional capability, Skill efficacy, generalization, and readiness remain unsupported.

exclusions: No other task has been authored against this intended construct and intended use.
Synthetic calibration cases are grader conformance cases, not independent suite items.
Invalid repository-escaping attempts and the provider-failed no-skill arm are excluded from capability and treatment estimates.

lineage_clusters.basis: Task evidence, traps, and procedural requirements derive primarily from one LH-Bench review.

intended_mixture.difficulty: A future portfolio with varied evidence authority, contradictions, artifacts, and decisions; no numeric target is yet validated.

realized_mixture.assessment: Convenience one-task pilot; intended mixture is not realized.

evidence_precision_target.target: No suite-level estimand or precision target has been established.

unresolved: All task content and calibration cases share source and builder lineage.

missing_invalid_policy.missing_artifact: invalid observation; do not score as task failure
missing_invalid_policy.grader_insufficient_evidence: abstain/fail closed; do not convert to substantive error

alternate_assembly_sensitivity.reason: A one-task eligible pool has no legitimate alternate assembly and cannot support ranking, coverage, or precision sensitivity.

suite_sufficiency.claim: This one-task pilot is sufficient to measure professional knowledge-work capability or Skill efficacy.
suite_sufficiency.reason: No sampled pool, validated mixture, precision target, alternate assembly, or cross-domain coverage exists.

claims.statement: The task, response representation, and checks are expert-valid for professional work.
claims.scope: No domain expert has approved the task, candidate procedures, response view, rubric, or thresholds.
prohibited_interpretations: A schema-valid crosswalk establishes content or ecological validity

claims.statement: The public procedural Skill causally improves performance relative to no Skill.
claims.scope: The v6 public-skill arm completed, while the no-skill arm failed at provider streaming and produced no deliverables.
prohibited_interpretations: One completed arm is a matched contrast
Provider failure is agent task failure
Internal grader calibration is a Skill-effect metric

claims.statement: The administered suite supplies sufficient evidence for the intended professional capability inference.
claims.scope: Exactly one convenience task from one source and builder lineage.
prohibited_interpretations: One healthy task establishes suite coverage
Calibration fixtures increase independent task count
Equal task weighting supplies occupational or economic representativeness

claims.statement: Results generalize across knowledge-work domains, professions, artifacts, and configured systems.
claims.scope: No cross-domain task or system sample exists.
prohibited_interpretations: Cross-domain reusable machinery is cross-domain empirical validity
One benchmark-management pilot represents the charter portfolio

claims.statement: The pilot or an evaluated agent is ready for benchmark release or professional deployment.
claims.scope: Release review is blocked and no deployment population, threshold, impact evidence, or residual-risk acceptance exists.
prohibited_interpretations: One valid configured-system execution establishes deployment value or safety

audit_limits: This is a builder-authored prospective checker calibration, not independent evidence that ECBD improves benchmark quality.
Artifact hashes and JSON Pointers establish record identity and connection, not truth, expert authority, construct validity, or causal identification.

The audit discovers an incomplete response-view warrant, an unimplemented expert check, a calibration-metric/professional-criterion mismatch, absent suite assembly evidence, and an incomplete matched treatment pair.
A later real expert-reviewed cross-domain pilot must measure reviewer agreement, time, defects found, claim contractions, and false alarms.
